rationale_for_baseline_tier: Inflammatory-pattern back pain with a negative cauda-equina screen and no radiculopathy is most likely an axial PsA flare — not an ED-by-ambulance emergency — but the spinal/neurological exam and MRI can't be done remotely, so urgent in-person review (± MRI) with cauda-equina safety-netting, NSAID optimisation and fall-safety (T3) is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for saddle numbness, sphincter disturbance or new leg weakness.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Saddle/groin numbness, loss of bladder/bowel control or difficulty passing urine, or new leg weakness
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicate possible cauda equina/cord compression — a surgical emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop numbness around the saddle area, lose control of your bladder or bowels, can't pass urine, or get new leg weakness — call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A fall, or being unable to transfer/reach the bathroom safely alone
escalation_edges.rationale: High fall risk with functional immobility while isolated.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you fall or can't safely get to the bathroom, arrange same-day help — call someone or an ambulance if needed.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with axial rigidity and floor hazards
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prioritise fall-safety (remove rugs), transfer aids and someone to help; consider ambulance if unsafe.
Optimise NSAID and arrange rheumatology review; MRI assesses active sacroiliitis.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like a flare of your axial psoriatic arthritis — inflammation around the spine and pelvis. We've checked the nerve danger signs and they're clear, but you still need an in-person review soon, possibly with an MRI, and your regular anti-inflammatory optimised. Use log-roll transfers, remove trip hazards, and get help moving so you don't fall.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent in-person GP review within 1–2 days (± MRI); use 000 for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Call 000 immediately for saddle numbness, bladder/bowel changes or new leg weakness; otherwise arrange urgent in-person review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the inflammatory-pattern axial flare with bilateral sacroiliitis, negative cauda-equina screen, maxed OTC NSAIDs, and the need for a neuro exam ± MRI and NSAID optimisation.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Managing/reassuring with no cauda-equina screen, missing a surgical emergency.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving cauda equina is missed and the patient is left with permanent paralysis/incontinence.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/000 for a screen-negative inflammatory flare, over-medicalising a manageable presentation.
